methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
KrattWorks is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Martin Karmin, with reported revenue of €19.7M. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 private and 2 public companies in the Defense & Security sector, including peers such as Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries at the same stage.

The peer-median method applied to three same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a central valuation of $300M. The peer sample ranges from $150M (Ares Industries) to $500M (Stark Defence), establishing a $350M spread. This method represents the only fully applicable triangulation approach given available data constraints.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $255M to $345M, centered on $300M. Key uncertainties include the limited sample size of directly comparable Series A defense companies and the absence of revenue multiples or growth metrics for peer normalization. The analysis does not incorporate public market multiples (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) due to stage and structural differences between public and pre-Series B private companies. Any refinement would require access to peer revenue figures, capital efficiency metrics, or customer concentration data for KrattWorks relative to named comparables.
